Title: Characterization and functional reconstruction of a highly productive germacrene A synthase from Liriodendron chinense

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A280 is critical for catalytic cyclization; A280V abolishes germacrene A cyclization and yields linear farnesol, while A280T causes complete loss of activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fragment swapping between LcTPS3 and LcTPS4; site-directed mutagenesis; GC-MS; molecular dynamics simulations
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fragment 270-280 was essential for LcTPS3 function. 2. A280T caused complete loss of activity. 3. A280V prevented cyclization and produced farnesol, indicating a key role in substrate conformation and cyclization readiness.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Germacrene A is a sesquiterpene scaffold and precursor of β-elemene
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of desired product formation upon mutation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful residue target for rational tuning of TPS cyclization outcomes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A280 is not a separate gene, but a decisive engineered feature directly analyzed in Results.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Catalytic triad residue within terpene synthase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Y531 is required for α-guaiene formation in the Y282L-reconstructed enzyme but not for basal germacrene A production
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis of Y531F and double mutant Y282L/Y531F; GC-MS; molecular modeling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Catalytic triad residue Y531 was implicated in secondary cyclization. 2. Y531F alone did not block germacrene A formation. 3. Y531F in the Y282L background abolished α-guaiene production, supporting Y531-mediated protonation in secondary ring closure.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: α-Guaiene is a precursor to rotundone aroma compound
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key design position for reprogramming secondary cyclization chemistry in TPS enzymes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as a mechanistic residue rather than an independent gene; included because it is experimentally decisive for metabolite outcome.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: P_GAL1-P_GAL10 (galactose-inducible) in comparison strains; glucose deprivation-inducible after GAL80 disruption in JLcTPS309
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS-a family); terpene_synth_C (PF03936) and terpene_synth (PF01397) domains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Germacrene A branch / central sesquiterpene scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal cyclization step from FPP to sesquiterpene scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl diphosphate (FPP) to (+)-germacrene A, which is heat-converted nonenzymatically to β-elemene during GC-MS or processing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: β-Elemene is a broad-spectrum antitumor drug used clinically; germacrene A is a central scaffold for diverse sesquiterpenes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and supplies precursor for β-elemene via heat-induced conversion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/terminal scaffold-forming step; high-productivity enzyme identified as major bottleneck-relieving catalyst for production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected from transcriptome-supported TPS candidates across various tissues; exact tissue for LcTPS3 not specified
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in engineered yeast during fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature (250°C GC-MS injector) converts germacrene A completely to β-elemene; enzyme optimum at 35°C and pH 7.5; fed-batch two-phase fermentation with isopropyl myristate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases germacrene A production; in yeast enabled record heterologous titer and indirect β-elemene production potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in S. cerevisiae and E. coli; GC-MS; product isolation and optical rotation; purified enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; metabolic engineering; fed-batch fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome BioProject PRJNA418360; transcriptome/RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA780974
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcTPS3 was identified from L. chinense TPS candidates as a highly productive germacrene A synthase. 2. Its apparent GC-MS product β-elemene was confirmed to originate from heat-converted (+)-germacrene A. 3. Purified LcTPS3 showed highest reported catalytic efficiency among compared plant GAS enzymes in the paper. 4. Expression of LcTPS3 in engineered yeast enabled 151.89 mg/L in shake flasks and 14.71 g/L in 5-L fed-batch fermentation after pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Thermal rearrangement complicates direct product detection; no explicit growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent enzyme for microbial production of germacrene A/β-elemene and for engineering other high-value sesquiterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sequence identity to previously known GAS enzymes is only 40-43%, indicating a divergent yet highly efficient GAS. Product F4 was initially misassigned as β-elemene by GC-MS because of thermal rearrangement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Alters LcTPS3 catalysis from exclusive germacrene A formation toward secondary cyclization producing α-guaiene from germacrenyl cation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor to rotundone, a key peppery aroma compound in wine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms alternative target product by diverting flux from germacrene A branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous mutant expression in yeast for product assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts product profile from germacrene A to α-guaiene through functional reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; GC-MS; product standard comparison; molecular docking; molecular dynamics; mutational interaction with Y531F
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Y282L created a new sesquiterpene product, α-guaiene. 2. Modeling suggested the mutation shortens the distance between catalytic residue Y531 and C5 of germacrene A/germacrenyl cation. 3. Y531F abolished α-guaiene formation in the Y282L background, supporting the proposed secondary cyclization mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce exclusive germacrene A production due to product switching
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates enzyme plasticity and utility for rational design of α-guaiene and other complex sesquiterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is an engineered mutant rather than a native gene, but it is a direct experimental core subject for metabolite redirection.
